name: Faro Matxitxako and Views to San Juan de Gaztelugatxe
name_original: Faro Matxitxako y Vistas a San Juan de Gaztelugatxe — circular por el Valle de Butrón
description: This difficult 50.4-mile (81.1 km) road cycling route offers stunning coastal views from Faro Matxitxako and San Juan de Gaztelugatxe.
region_intro: Road cycling around Derio features a diverse landscape characterized by coastal stretches, significant climbs, and views of urban centers like Bilbao. The terrain includes both challenging ascents to mountain passes and routes along the coast, offering varied gradients for road cyclists. The region's topography provides opportunities for routes with substantial elevation gain, often leading to panoramic viewpoints. Best road cycling routes around Derio The most popular road cycling route is Faro Matxitxako and Views to San Juan de Gaztelugatxe, a 50.4 miles (81.1 km) trail that takes 4 hours 33 minutes to complete. This route features significant elevation gain and coastal scenery, including views of the iconic San Juan de Gaztelugatxe. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Vizcaya Bridge – Puerto de Morga loop from Derio, a difficult 54.7 miles (88.1 km) path. This route combines a coastal landmark with a challenging climb to Puerto de Morga, offering varied terrain and views. Local road cyclists also love the Puerto de Morga – Climb From Goikoeleixalde loop from Derio, a 25.4 miles (40.9 km) trail leading through hilly terrain and offering a climb to Puerto de Morga, often completed in about 2 hours 4 minutes. Road cycling around Derio is defined by coastal paths, challenging climbs to mountain passes, and routes offering views of the surrounding region. The network provides options for various ability levels, from moderate loops to advanced road cycling trails with significant elevation. The routes in Derio are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 500 reviews. More than 29,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Derio's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Derio?
A: There are over 230 road cycling routes around Derio, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Derio?
A: Yes, Derio offers a selection of routes suitable for beginners. While the region is known for its challenging climbs, there are 33 routes classified as easy. For those looking for a moderate introduction, there are also 107 moderate routes available.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Derio?
A: Road cycling around Derio features a diverse landscape, including coastal stretches, significant climbs to mountain passes, and routes offering views of urban centers like Bilbao. You'll encounter varied gradients, from challenging ascents to more rolling coastal paths.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling routes in Derio?
A: While many routes in Derio feature significant elevation, some of the shorter, moderate routes can be suitable for families with older children or those comfortable with some climbing. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39565275" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Covered Bridge in Bilbao – View of Bilbao loop from Ola&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 26.9 km route that offers scenic views without extreme difficulty.

Q: What are some challenging road cycling routes for advanced riders in Derio?
A: Advanced riders will find plenty of challenging options. The region features 90 difficult routes with substantial elevation gain. A notable example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1032150182/faro-matxitxako-and-views-to-san-juan-de-gaztelugatxe" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Faro Matxitxako and Views to San Juan de Gaztelugatxe&lt;/a&gt;, a 81.1 km route with over 1700m of climbing, offering coastal scenery and views of San Juan de Gaztelugatxe.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes around Derio?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around Derio are designed as loops. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38150478" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vizcaya Bridge – Puerto de Morga loop from Derio&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 88.1 km circular path combining coastal landmarks with a challenging climb. Another option is the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39565876" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Bilbao – Climb to Côte de Pike loop from Sondika&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are the best times of year for road biking in Derio?
A: The coastal influence means Derio generally experiences mild winters and warm summers. Spring and autumn often provide the most pleasant conditions for road cycling, with moderate temperatures and less extreme weather, making it ideal for tackling both coastal and mountain pass routes.

Q: What scenic viewpoints can I find along road cycling routes in Derio?
A: Many routes offer panoramic views, especially those that climb to mountain passes or follow the coastline. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1032150182/faro-matxitxako-and-views-to-san-juan-de-gaztelugatxe" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Faro Matxitxako and Views to San Juan de Gaztelugatxe&lt;/a&gt; provide stunning coastal vistas. Routes near Bilbao also offer urban panoramas, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39565876" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Bilbao – Climb to Côte de Pike loop from Sondika&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What interesting landmarks or attractions can I see near the road cycling routes?
A: The region around Derio is rich in attractions. Many routes pass near or offer views of significant landmarks. You might encounter the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3902698" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vizcaya Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, or have views towards the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/335425" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guggenheim Museum&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2911339" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zubizuri Bridge&lt;/a&gt; in Bilbao. For natural beauty, routes can lead to areas with views of peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5322343" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mount Pagasarri&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any hidden gem road bike routes near Derio?
A: While specific 'hidden gems' are often discovered by local exploration, routes that venture into the less-trafficked mountain passes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31620762" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Morga – Climb From Goikoeleixalde loop from Derio&lt;/a&gt;, can offer a more secluded cycling experience through hilly terrain away from the main coastal paths.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Derio?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 500 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse landscape, which combines challenging climbs with beautiful coastal scenery, and the opportunity to experience both natural beauty and urban views.

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty of this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as difficult, requiring very good fitness due to its significant elevation gain of 1,743 meters over approximately 81 kilometers. It features sustained climbs, including the notable Cadiot Climb.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: The terrain primarily consists of well-paved asphalt roads (about 79.7 km) and other paved surfaces, making it ideal for road bikes. While mostly paved, some short segments might be unpaved or difficult, potentially requiring you to push your bike.

faqs.question: What are the main scenic highlights along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll experience diverse and spectacular scenery. Key highlights include the Matxitxako Lighthouse, offering expansive 270-degree coastal views, and stunning Views of San Juan de Gaztelugatxe. The route also passes through the green landscapes of the Butrón Valley and includes a Scenic Road to Maruri with a fast descent.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this circular road cycling route?
faqs.answer: As this is a circular route, you can typically find parking in nearby towns that serve as good starting points, such as Bermeo or Mungia. There isn't one specific designated trailhead parking for the entire loop, so look for public parking options in these areas.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Basque Country generally offers pleasant cycling conditions from late spring through early autumn (May to October). During these months, you'll likely encounter more stable weather, less rain, and comfortable temperatures for tackling the demanding climbs and enjoying the coastal views.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to cycle this trail, especially within the Urdaibai Biosphere Reserve?
faqs.answer: No specific cycling permits or entrance fees are required for this route or for cycling within the Urdaibai Biosphere Reserve. However, always respect local regulations, stay on marked paths, and protect the natural environment of this protected area.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This is a challenging road cycling route, and bringing dogs along on a bike can be difficult and potentially unsafe, especially given the significant climbs and descents. While there are no explicit bans, it's generally not recommended for dogs due to the nature of the sport and the route's demands.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling paths or trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route intersect with other official trails. You will encounter parts of the Uribe (11%), Bizkaiko bira (10%), and [CIMA BI05] Sollube * Bermeo (7%) trails, among others, along the way.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or drink along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, there are options for refreshments. The Sollube Cannon Etxea Bar Restaurant is a notable highlight along the route where you can stop for a break and refuel.

faqs.question: What should I pack for this challenging road cycling tour?
faqs.answer: Given the route's difficulty and length, pack essentials like plenty of water and snacks, appropriate cycling gear (helmet, padded shorts), a repair kit, sun protection, and layers of clothing for changing weather. A GPS device is also highly recommended for navigation.

faqs.question: Can I get views of San Juan de Gaztelugatxe from the route?
faqs.answer: Absolutely! The route offers stunning Views of San Juan de Gaztelugatxe, particularly from the area around the Matxitxako Lighthouse and the Matxitxako Viewpoint. You'll have excellent opportunities to see this iconic islet.

waypoints.name: Views of San Juan de Gaztelugatxe
